audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for divine remembering, wind over waters, ark resting, raven/dove sequence, altar, pleasing aroma, and the promise of ongoing creation rhythms. Final source-critical review may refine chronology details.

verses.restored: And the Divine remembered Noah, and every living being and every animal that was with him in the ark. And the Divine caused a wind to pass over the earth, and the waters subsided.

familiar.text: And God remembered Noah, and every living thing... and God made a wind to pass over the earth, and the waters asswaged;

notes.title: Remembered
notes.body: Divine remembering is covenantal attention, not recovery from forgetfulness.

notes.title: Wind over waters
notes.body: Ruach over the flood waters echoes Genesis 1:2, suggesting re-creation.

verses.restored: The fountains of the deep and the windows of the heavens were closed, and the rain from the heavens was restrained.

familiar.text: The fountains also of the deep and the windows of heaven were stopped, and the rain from heaven was restrained;

verses.restored: The waters returned from upon the earth, going and returning, and at the end of one hundred fifty days the waters diminished.

familiar.text: And the waters returned from off the earth continually: and after the end of the hundred and fifty days the waters were abated.

verses.restored: In the seventh month, on the seventeenth day of the month, the ark rested upon the mountains of Ararat.

familiar.text: And the ark rested in the seventh month... upon the mountains of Ararat.

notes.title: Rested
notes.body: Nuach, rested, resonates with Noah's name and the longing for rest from cursed-ground toil.

verses.restored: The waters kept diminishing until the tenth month; in the tenth month, on the first of the month, the tops of the mountains appeared.

familiar.text: And the waters decreased continually until the tenth month... were the tops of the mountains seen.

notes.title: Mountains appear
notes.body: The reappearance of land echoes the emergence of dry ground in Genesis 1.

verses.restored: At the end of forty days, Noah opened the window of the ark that he had made.

familiar.text: And it came to pass at the end of forty days, that Noah opened the window of the ark which he had made:

verses.restored: And he sent out the raven, and it went out, going out and returning, until the waters dried from upon the earth.

familiar.text: And he sent forth a raven, which went forth to and fro, until the waters were dried up from off the earth.

verses.restored: Then he sent out the dove from him, to see whether the waters had lessened from the face of the ground.

familiar.text: Also he sent forth a dove from him, to see if the waters were abated from off the face of the ground;

verses.restored: But the dove found no resting place for the sole of her foot, and she returned to him into the ark, for waters were over the face of all the earth. He sent out his hand and took her and brought her to himself into the ark.

familiar.text: But the dove found no rest for the sole of her foot, and she returned unto him into the ark...

notes.title: No resting place
notes.body: Rest language continues the Noah/rest theme. The new world is not yet ready for dwelling.

verses.restored: He waited yet another seven days, and again sent out the dove from the ark.

familiar.text: And he stayed yet other seven days; and again he sent forth the dove out of the ark;

verses.restored: The dove came to him at evening, and look: in her mouth was a freshly plucked olive leaf. So Noah knew that the waters had lessened from upon the earth.

familiar.text: And the dove came in to him in the evening; and, lo, in her mouth was an olive leaf pluckt off...

notes.title: Olive leaf
notes.body: The fresh leaf signals returning life after judgment.

verses.restored: He waited yet another seven days and sent out the dove, and she did not return to him again.

familiar.text: And he stayed yet other seven days; and sent forth the dove; which returned not again unto him any more.

verses.restored: In the six hundred first year, in the first month, on the first of the month, the waters were dried from upon the earth. Noah removed the covering of the ark and looked, and behold, the face of the ground was dry.

familiar.text: And it came to pass in the six hundredth and first year... Noah removed the covering of the ark...

verses.restored: In the second month, on the twenty-seventh day of the month, the earth was dry.

familiar.text: And in the second month, on the seven and twentieth day of the month, was the earth dried.

verses.restored: And the Divine spoke to Noah, saying,

familiar.text: And God spake unto Noah, saying,

verses.restored: Go out from the ark, you and your woman, your sons and your sons’ women with you.

familiar.text: Go forth of the ark, thou, and thy wife, and thy sons, and thy sons' wives with thee.

verses.restored: Bring out with you every living being that is with you of all flesh: winged creatures, animals, and every creeping thing that creeps upon the earth. Let them swarm on the earth, be fruitful, and multiply upon the earth.

familiar.text: Bring forth with thee every living thing... that they may breed abundantly in the earth, and be fruitful, and multiply upon the earth.

notes.title: Creation blessing renewed
notes.body: Be fruitful and multiply echoes Genesis 1, making the ark exit a renewal of creation.

verses.restored: So Noah went out, and his sons, his woman, and his sons’ women with him.

familiar.text: And Noah went forth, and his sons, and his wife, and his sons' wives with him:

verses.restored: Every living being, every creeping thing, every winged creature, everything moving upon the earth, went out from the ark by their families.

familiar.text: Every beast, every creeping thing, and every fowl... went forth out of the ark.

verses.restored: And Noah built an altar to the Eternal. He took from every clean animal and every clean winged creature and offered burnt offerings on the altar.

familiar.text: And Noah builded an altar unto the LORD... and offered burnt offerings on the altar.

notes.title: Altar after preservation
notes.body: Noah’s first recorded act after leaving the ark is worship.

verses.restored: And the Eternal smelled the pleasing aroma. And the Eternal said in his heart, I will never again curse the ground because of the human, for the shaping of the human heart is harmful from youth. Neither will I again strike down every living thing as I have done.

familiar.text: And the LORD smelled a sweet savour; and the LORD said in his heart, I will not again curse the ground...

notes.title: Said in his heart
notes.body: The text presents an inner divine resolve after the offering.

notes.title: Human heart
notes.body: The human heart remains bent toward harm from youth, yet the Divine pledges restraint rather than repeated total destruction.

verses.restored: As long as the earth remains: seedtime and harvest, cold and heat, summer and winter, day and night shall not cease.

familiar.text: While the earth remaineth, seedtime and harvest, and cold and heat, and summer and winter, and day and night shall not cease.

notes.title: Creation rhythms preserved
notes.body: The promise preserves the cycles of earth. Judgment gives way to stable rhythms.
